slides.title: The *position* you sleep in changes your night.
slides.sub: How you lie down shapes your breathing, your reflux and your overnight oxygen. Here is what side sleeping can do.

slides.title: Position guides your airway
slides.body: On your back, the tongue and soft tissue can settle backward. Sleeping on your side *helps keep the airway open* through the night.

slides.title: Fewer pauses in breathing
slides.body: Back sleeping can worsen snoring and apnea events. For many people, the side position *is linked to* fewer interruptions.

slides.title: Calmer digestion at night
slides.body: Lying flat lets stomach acid drift upward. Sleeping on your left side *may help* reduce nighttime reflux and heartburn.

slides.title: Steadier oxygen overnight
slides.body: Smoother breathing means steadier oxygen while you sleep, which *can support* deeper, more restorative rest and clearer mornings.

slides.title: Set up the side
slides.body: A pillow behind your back or between your knees can keep you there. Small setup, *meaningful difference* over a full night.

slides.title: See how your sleep *adds up*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores your sleep, food, movement and steps in one daily number, so you can see how better nights show up in how you feel.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The way you sleep is quietly working for you or against you all night. 🌙

Side sleeping can help keep your airway open, ease reflux and steady your overnight oxygen for deeper rest. Save this for tonight. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your sleep and daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Side Sleeping Beats Sleeping on Your Back
